summary.intent: Refactor gossamer from an Electron desktop app into a VS Code extension to reduce install friction and improve context-switching between editing code and viewing Claude sessions.
summary.outcome: Successfully built a working VS Code extension with a sessions list, session detail view (with compact full-width message rows), a native VS Code tree view for checkpoints, and a native diff view using an in-memory TextDocumentContentProvider — all integrated with VS Code theming.

repo: Gossamer architecture: Entire.io CLI writes session data to a git branch, gossamer reads and indexes it into SQLite via Prisma/better-sqlite3, then serves it via an Express server
Detection of Entire.io-enabled repos is done by checking for .entire/settings.json at the repo root
The CheckpointIdGitOidJoin table maps checkpointId → gitOid; git show is used to retrieve file content at before/after states
The /api/v2/checkpoints/:id/file endpoint must look up the correct repoDir from the session's cwd, not use the extension's configured workspace — checkpoints can belong to different repos
npm workspace name is 'gossamer-vscode' not 'vscode', so -w flag requires 'gossamer-vscode'
packages/core prisma import path from src/ files is ../prisma/generated/client/index.js (one level up), not ../../prisma/...

code.finding: Must use 'include': ['src/**/*.ts'] with 'exclude': ['src/webview'] — listing individual files causes new files like SessionDetailPanel.ts and diffUtils.ts to silently not compile

Must use execFileSync('which', ['node']) to get system node path — process.execPath points to VS Code's bundled Electron Node.js which has a different NODE_MODULE_VERSION than better-sqlite3 was compiled for
Add serverReady flag + onDidChangeViewState listener to re-send server_ready when panel becomes visible — webview HTML reloads from scratch when tab is moved to a new window

Auto-scroll must check position inline at effect time (scrollHeight - scrollTop - clientHeight < 80), not via a separate scroll listener — Mantine ScrollArea viewport ref is null when the [] dep effect runs
root and outDir must be absolute paths using resolve(__dirname, ...) — relative paths resolve from CWD which is repo root when run via npm workspaces, not the package directory
Script tag must have type='module' — Vite outputs ESM and without type=module the browser silently ignores the script

Use TextDocumentContentProvider with gossamer-diff: URI scheme to serve diff content in-memory instead of writing to temp files — vscode.diff only accepts Uri objects but content providers make the documents inherently read-only

workflow: VS Code webview postMessage bridge: webview uses acquireVsCodeApi().postMessage() (wrapped in singleton), extension host uses panel.webview.onDidReceiveMessage — replace HTTP round-trips with this for extension-host actions
For native module (better-sqlite3) version mismatches in VS Code extensions: spawn a child process using system node instead of importing the module directly in the extension host

VS Code CSS variables (--vscode-editor-background, --vscode-panel-border, --vscode-button-background etc.) are automatically injected into webview body; body also gets vscode-light/vscode-dark class for Mantine forceColorScheme
vscode.diff command requires Uri objects; TextDocumentContentProvider with a custom scheme is the clean in-memory alternative to writing temp files

TreeDataProvider checkpoints sidebar: register in contributes.views in package.json, implement TreeDataProvider, call window.createTreeView, update with treeDataProvider.refresh() when session changes
Server readiness polling: poll GET /api/sessions every 500ms with 30s timeout; only post server_ready to webview after confirmed up — schema push on startup takes several seconds

friction: tsconfig.extension.json only listed two files in 'include' — SessionDetailPanel.ts and diffUtils.ts compiled to nothing, causing blank diff views with no error messages
VS Code's bundled Electron Node.js has a different NODE_MODULE_VERSION than the system Node.js better-sqlite3 was compiled for — required spawning a child process as a workaround
Vite relative root path resolved from CWD (repo root) rather than package dir when run via workspaces — error message 'Cannot resolve entry module index.html' was not obvious
Content provider approach for diff URIs was tried first but abandoned due to URI encoding fragility before being successfully re-implemented
git show returning empty silently because the endpoint used the wrong repoDir (extension workspace) instead of the session's cwd — required DB investigation to diagnose
Mantine ScrollArea viewport ref is null at [] dep effect time, making scroll listener attachment unreliable
npm workspace name gossamer-vscode caused -w flag confusion when trying to run build commands
open_items: Electron app is intentionally broken/deprioritized during this refactor — needs either fixing or formal deprecation
packages/core CLI tool (gossamer-index) deferred — only module API exposed for now
better-sqlite3 native binary rebuild for VS Code extension host NODE_MODULE_VERSION — documented as known issue but not resolved (worked around by spawning child process)
VS Code marketplace review process and publishing story not addressed
Daemon/background capture mode for when VS Code is closed — deferred to v2, currently only captures when VS Code is open
Electron app screens beyond sessions list not ported to VS Code extension webview
